content: Just wrapped an incredible project. The energy on set was unreal — everyone brought their absolute best. Grateful to be surrounded by such talent. Big things coming. 🙏
Quick reminder that mental health is something we all need to prioritise. Take a break today. You deserve it. Be kind to yourself. 💙
Art is about vulnerability. When I step on stage, I bare my soul. That raw connection with the audience — that's everything to me.
Surround yourself with people who inspire you to be better. Your environment shapes who you become. Choose wisely. 🙌
New chapter, same values. Excited for what's ahead and grateful for everything that brought me here. The journey continues.
my dog ate my AirPods. she seems fine. I am not fine.
my flight got delayed 4 hours and I've eaten nothing but airport pretzels since 6am. absolutely thriving
just watched myself on a talk show from 2019. I had a completely different personality. who was that person. what happened to them
OK who let me agree to an 8am interview. I look like a haunted house.
my assistant booked me on a red-eye the night before a 7am press junket and I simply cannot be held responsible for what I say in those interviews
We must come together as a nation to address the challenges that face us. By working across the aisle, we can build a future where every American has a fair shot at success.
Our children deserve the best education we can provide. Investing in schools is investing in America's future. That is a priority I will fight for every single day.
Leadership means making tough choices for the greater good, even when it's unpopular. That's what the people elected me to do.
"The situation is complex and evolving. We are committed to full transparency and will provide updates as they become available." — official spokesperson
We stand at a crossroads. The decisions we make today will echo for generations to come. I am committed to making the right ones.
Just got heckled at my own press conference by a guy in a hot dog costume. Democracy is beautiful.
The bill passed 51-49. My speech lasted 45 minutes and changed exactly zero votes. Love this job.
Spent the morning reviewing the 847-page infrastructure bill. Page 612 is where things get interesting.
Town hall in 10 minutes. The parking lot is already full of people holding signs. Should be a fun Tuesday.
Constituent asked me if I could fix their neighbour's fence. Checked. I cannot. But I did call the relevant department. They also cannot.
Hard work, dedication, and belief in your team — that's what champions are made of. Proud of everyone who contributed to this victory. The journey continues.
Every setback is a setup for a comeback. I've faced adversity before and I'll face it again. What matters is how you respond.
Season is over but the grind never stops. Off-season is where champions are made. See you in training camp. 💪
The team showed incredible character today. We didn't give up, we stayed the course, and we got the result we deserved.
Grateful for the support of every single fan. Your energy pushes us to give everything we have on that court every game.
Threw up before the game. Threw up after. We won so I guess it's fine
The ref cost us that game. I said what I said.
Missed the game-winning shot and my coach sent me a 47-second voice note. Working through it.
Airport security pulled me aside because my protein powder looked suspicious. Fourth time this year.
Tried to explain to my dog why we lost. She did not care. She is right.
Excited to announce that our team has been working on something that will fundamentally change the way we think about productivity. More details coming soon. Watch this space. 🚀
Innovation doesn't happen by accident. It takes a culture of curiosity, a tolerance for failure, and relentless execution. That's what we're building here. #startup #leadership #innovation

5 lessons after 10 years in tech: 1. Listen more than you speak. 2. Data beats intuition. 3. Culture eats strategy for breakfast. 4. Trust takes years to build and seconds to destroy. 5. Your network is your net worth. Like and repost if this resonated. 💼
The future of AI is not about replacing humans — it's about empowering them. That's why we build with intention, ethics, and humanity at the forefront of everything we do.
The best time to start building was yesterday. The second best time is today. Don't wait for the perfect moment. Start now.
our website went down for 40 minutes because someone pushed a semicolon to prod on a friday. it's always a friday.
spent 3 hours debugging only to discover the issue was a typo in a comment. the comment. a comment.
our 'AI-powered' feature is literally just three IF statements and two of our engineers are too scared to tell anyone
just had a one-hour meeting that could have been a slack message. which could have been nothing.
Hot take: most 'thought leadership' posts on this platform are just people saying obvious things in bullet points to get engagement. Including this one. Have a great Thursday.
Kindness is free. Sprinkle it everywhere. The world needs more of it today. Be the change you wish to see. 💫
Take a moment today to appreciate how far you've come. Growth isn't always linear, but every single step forward matters. You've got this.
Monday mindset: Show up. Do the work. Be grateful. Repeat. The compound effect of small daily habits is extraordinary. 💪

I've been meditating every morning for 30 days and the results are genuinely life-changing. My focus is sharper, my anxiety is lower, and I feel more present in every moment. Highly recommend to anyone who's been on the fence.
You are exactly where you need to be right now. Trust the timing of your life. The right opportunities will come to those who stay ready and keep showing up.
accidentally sent a meme to my entire company instead of just my friend. the meme was about hating mondays. it was monday morning.
the smoke alarm went off while I was making toast. my neighbor called the fire department. there is no toast. there is no dignity.
tried to parallel park in front of a driving school. did not succeed. several students were watching. one clapped.

I got up to give my seat to an elderly person on the subway. someone else immediately sat down. I ended up standing and so did the elderly person who never asked in the first place. three of us are now standing.
my uber driver asked if I'd heard of this great new podcast. it was my own podcast. I said yes, it's pretty good.
